zh.lifeSummary: 當Olive Abigail Randall於大約1796年出生在美國緬因州約克素斯 貝維其可時，她的父親Stephen Randall39歲，母親Lydia Olive Abbott25歲。她於1820年11月21日與Samuel Lemuel Drew在美國緬因州約克貝維其可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1850年住在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納。她去世於1870年9月4日在美國緬因州約克素斯 貝維其可享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Olive Abigail Randall于大约1796年出生在美国缅因州約克素斯 貝維其可时，她的父亲Stephen Randall39岁，母亲Lydia Olive Abbott25岁。她于1820年11月21日与Samuel Lemuel Drew在美国缅因州約克貝維其可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她于1850年住在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納。她去世于1870年9月4日在美国缅因州約克素斯 貝維其可享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randell
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German   Randel  .

variants.name: Randon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.
English: probably a variant of Randall , with assimilation of n-l to n-n.
French (northern): from the ancient Germanic personal name Rando, a short form of names based on the element rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’.

variants.name: Randol
meanings: English: altered form of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randal
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Randal in Gulen, a compound of Old Norse runnr ‘copse, thicket, grove’ + dalr ‘valley’.

variants.name: Randles
meanings: English and Irish (Louth): variant of Randall , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland this is sometimes a variant of Reynolds .

variants.name: Randle
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German Randel or of its Bavarian variant Randl.

variants.name: Rendell
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Dorset): variant of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randel
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
South German: variant of   Rand   3.

variants.name: Randolph
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rendall
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Scottish: habitational name from Rendall (Orkney), named with a stream-name of uncertain origin + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Swedish   Rendahl  .

nameMeanings.name: Olive
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a medieval given name, Oliff(e), derived via French from the Late Latin name Oliva, which was borne by two obscure early saints. It in turn is derived from the Latin name for the olive tree, and was no doubt adopted at first because of the associations of the olive tree with peaceful productivity and fruitfulness; the olive branch has been a symbol of peace since biblical times. Like many names denoting plants and flowers, it was particularly popular in the early decades of the 20th century. See also Olivia .

nameMeanings.name: Abigail
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘father of exaltation’ in Hebrew, borne by one of King David's wives, who had earlier been married to Nabal (1 Samuel 25:3), and by the mother of Absalom's captain Amasa (2 Samuel 1:25). The name first came into general use in Britain in the 16th century, under Puritan influence. It was a common name in literature for a lady's maid, for example in  Beaumont  and  Fletcher's  play The Scornful Lady (  1616 ). The biblical Abigail refers to herself as ‘thy servant’ in addressing King David. In Ireland this name has traditionally been used as an Anglicized form of Gobnat , although the reasons for this are not clear. It was popular in the 17th century, especially among Puritans and Nonconformists, and has again enjoyed considerable favour since the 1990s.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Historical Boundaries: 1814: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.
Historical Boundaries: 1839: Aroostook, Maine, United States
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

en.lifeSummary: When Olive Abigail Randall was born about 1796, in South Berwick, York, Maine, United States, her father, Stephen Randall, was 39 and her mother, Lydia Olive Abbott, was 25. She married Samuel Lemuel Drew on 21 November 1820, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, United States in 1850. She died on 4 September 1870, in South Berwick, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 75.

Elle a épousé Samuel Lemuel Drew le 21 novembre 1820, à Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle a vécu à Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis en 1850. Elle est décédée le 4 septembre 1870, à South Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 75 ans.

Sie hat am 21. November 1820 in Berwick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten Samuel Lemuel Drew geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1850 hat sie in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 4. September 1870 im Alter von 75 Jahren in South Berwick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Olive Abigail Randall è nata il circa 1796 nel South Berwick, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Stephen Randall, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Lydia Olive Abbott, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Samuel Lemuel Drew il 21 novembre 1820, nel Berwick, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850. Morì il 4 settembre 1870, nel South Berwick, York, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Olive Abigail Randallが約1796年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークソウス バーウィックで生まれた時，父Stephen Randallは39歳，母Lydia Olive Abbottは25歳であった。1820年11月21日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックでSamuel Lemuel Drewと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクスムイルナに住んでいた。彼女は1870年9月4日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークソウス バーウィックで75歳で死去した。

ko.North Berwick, York, Maine, United State: 미국 메인 주 요르크 노르스 버윅
ko.South Berwick, York, Maine, United State: 미국 메인 주 요르크 소우스 버윅
ko.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine: 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭
ko.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United S: 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭
ko.lifeSummary: Olive Abigail Randall(이)가 대략 1796년 미국 메인 주 요르크 소우스 버윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Stephen Randall(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lydia Olive Abbott(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1820년 11월 21일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 Samuel Lemuel Drew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 스미른아에서 살았다. 그녀는 1870년 9월 4일 미국 메인 주 요르크 소우스 버윅에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Olive Abigail Randall nasceu aproximadamente 1796, em South Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Stephen Randall, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Lydia Olive Abbott, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Samuel Lemuel Drew em 21 de novembro de 1820, em Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ela faleceu em 4 de setembro de 1870, em South Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Olive Abigail Randall родилась около 1796 г. в местности соут бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Stephen Randall было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lydia Olive Abbott, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Samuel Lemuel Drew 21 ноября 1820 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности смирна, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла 4 сентября 1870 г. в местности соут бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Olive Abigail Randall nació aproximadamente 1796, en South Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, su papá, Stephen Randall, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Lydia Olive Abbott, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Samuel Lemuel Drew el 21 de noviembre de 1820, en Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió el 4 de septiembre de 1870, en South Berwick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 75 años.
